reason: This passage (Genesis 29-31) is a narrative account of Jacob's arrival in Paddan-aram, his marriages, the births of his children, his business arrangements with Laban, and his eventual departure with his family. While the text includes a warning from God preventing Laban from harming Jacob, there is no prophecy or announcement of an upcoming destruction—whether man-made, natural, or supernatural—by Yahweh. The only reference to potential destruction is Laban's capacity to harm Jacob (31:29), which is explicitly prevented by divine warning, not announced as an impending catastrophe. The passage contains no reference to any disaster affecting the general public.
